title: for, while and loop control

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for for, while and loop control; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: The Python Tutorial; Python 3.14; Tutorial chapters 3-5: values, control flow, and data structures; atomic topic: for, while and loop control
sources.claim: Python for loops request values from an iterable, while loops repeat from a condition; correct loops define progress, termination, and behavior for empty input without needless manual indexing.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Python for loops request values from an iterable, while loops repeat from a condition; correct loops define progress, termination, and behavior for empty input without needless manual indexing.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A for loop sums values directly and enumerate supplies indices only when both position and value are part of the output. Your goal is to implement and debug collection, counted and sentinel loops without needless index manipulation. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Scripts, indentation and main guard; pf state expressions; if and match. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: Python for loops request values from an iterable, while loops repeat from a condition; correct loops define progress, termination, and behavior for empty input without needless manual indexing. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: range excludes its stop, mutating a container during iteration can skip or duplicate work, loop else has no-break semantics, and an exhausted iterator cannot restart itself. The learner must reject this documented misconception: Python for always increments an integer index and range includes both endpoints.

includes: Iterate over values directly and use condition-controlled loops with explicit progress.
Implement and debug collection, counted and sentinel loops without needless index manipulation.
excludes: range excludes its stop, mutating a container during iteration can skip or duplicate work, loop else has no-break semantics, and an exhausted iterator cannot restart itself.

outcomes: Implement and debug collection, counted and sentinel loops without needless index manipulation.

examples.title: for, while and loop control: direct language case
examples.situation: A for loop sums values directly and enumerate supplies indices only when both position and value are part of the output.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A for loop sums values directly and enumerate supplies indices only when both position and value are part of the output.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: A for loop consumes an iterable, and range excludes its stop; select direct iteration first and prove while-loop progress toward termination.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because A for loop consumes an iterable, and range excludes its stop; select direct iteration first and prove while-loop progress toward termination.

examples.title: for, while and loop control: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A while loop reads until a sentinel or end of input and updates state on every error path so invalid data cannot trap execution.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A while loop reads until a sentinel or end of input and updates state on every error path so invalid data cannot trap execution.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: range excludes its stop, mutating a container during iteration can skip or duplicate work, loop else has no-break semantics, and an exhausted iterator cannot restart itself.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: Python for always increments an integer index and range includes both endpoints.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Implement and debug collection, counted and sentinel loops without needless index manipulation.
